steps.title: Apply the parallel-lines angle rules
steps.content: When a transversal cuts two parallel lines, corresponding angles are equal, alternate interior angles are equal, and same-side interior angles are supplementary (sum to $180^{\circ}$).

steps.title: Solve for $x$
steps.content:
Transversal $t$ cuts parallel lines $r$ and $s$. The $105^{\circ}$ angle at the intersection with $r$ (above $r$, left of $t$) and angle $x^{\circ}$ at the intersection with $s$ (above $s$, left of $t$) are corresponding angles. Corresponding angles are equal, so:
$$x = 105$$
That confirms D is correct.
